title: The Greenhouse is overflowing, and now it pays
summary:
A massive influx of community apps, a new endpoint for programmatic model health checks, and a fix for Azure's overzealous content filters.

Thirteen new community apps just dropped into the directory. Highlights include PolliCross (dynamically generating playable Picross puzzles from AI images), Storiva (inpainting kids' faces into storybooks), and Generative Soundscape (endless text-to-music). More importantly, the `app_listed` quest is now live. Ship an app, list it in the public directory, and the system automatically drops Pollen into your wallet.

We added a `GET /v1/models/status` endpoint. It proxies our Tinybird telemetry with a 60-second cache, letting you query uptime and health data (configurable from 1 to 1440 minutes) directly. You can now programmatically check if a model is having a bad day before routing requests to it.

Azure's eastus2 endpoint started throwing false-positive content policy errors for perfectly innocent `gptimage` prompts, so we repointed the models to westus3 to bypass the filters. We also added a middleware filter to silently strip the `reasoning_effort` parameter when you hit non-reasoning models (like mistral-small-3.2 or qwen-vision), preventing 400 errors when you're reusing global request configs across different endpoints.
